_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Integrate and align information security and/or cybersecurity policies to ensure system analysis meets security requirements.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to design security and privacy architectures that enable data utility and AI innovation while maintaining protection levels appropriate to the data sensitivity and regulatory context.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-T-001 (SP, cosine: 0.531), RC-A-005 (RC, cosine: 0.523).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.9/10 (raw 0.686). Shared vocabulary: [security]. DCWF-only terms [align, analysis, cybersecurity, ensure, information, integrate] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, ai, appropriate, architectures, context, data] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.868 (P=0.870, R=0.865). Balanced P/R — the 15-word DCWF element and 26-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (92.8%). "Integrate and align information security and/or cybersecurity policies to ensure" and "Ability to design security and privacy architectures that enable data utility an" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.533 < STS 0.686 (gap -0.153). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [align, analysis, cybersecurity] and [ability, ai, appropriate] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 9.1%. Shared tokens: [security]. DCWF-only: [align, analysis, cybersecurity, ensure, information, integrate]. WIDAI-only: [ability, ai, appropriate, architectures, context, data].
